message:
fix(grpc): make RequestSink::send atomic — failed hooks no longer increment sent_count

Previously, RequestSink::send incremented sent_count before calling the
on_send hook. If the hook returned an error, the count was already bumped,
causing a discrepancy between the count and actually-sent messages.

The fix splits the fast path (no hook) and hooked path:
- No hook: check closed, store message and increment count atomically
  under a single lock acquisition, then return
- With hook: check closed under lock, release lock, call hook, then
  only increment count under a second lock if the hook succeeded

This ensures sent_count is a faithful count of messages that were
actually accepted by the full send pipeline.

Tests added:
- request_sink_failed_send_hook_does_not_increment_sent_count
- request_sink_successful_send_hook_increments_sent_count

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
